title: plagiospermum benzoin
intro: Plagiospermum benzoin is a plant species in the family Rutaceae, also known as the citrus or rue family, native to the tropical regions of Australia and Southeast Asia. The plant's resinous sap has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of Plagiospermum benzoin are attributed to its ability to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis. It can be prepared for medicinal use through herbal preparations such as infusions, tinctures, and salves, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
